figures.caption: Figure 2: Schematic EnVar loop: ensemble generation, forward propagation, optimisation, optimum update and iteration
Figure 4: Spectrum and leading eigenvectors of the covariance matrix B. The eigenvectors define the dominant directions retained in the ensemble subspace.
Figure 5: Re = 6250. Streamwise velocity component of mean flow 5a; vorticity field 5b.
Figure 6: Characterization of the quasi-periodic dynamic.
Figure 7: Evolution and decomposition of the cost function for the EnVar Gauss–Newton (−), the EnVar L-BFGS-B (− · −), and the adjoint-based optimisation algorithm (· · · )..
Figure 8: Optimisation of the forcing shape, adjoint(a), EnVar Gauss–Newton(b), EnVar L-BFGS-B(c).

Figure 9: Comparative characterization of quasi-periodic flow dynamics under controlled and uncontrolled conditions. The uncontrolled flow is shown in blue, whereas the controlled flow obtained with the Gauss–Newton EnVar method is shown in red. The time evolution of the global kinetic energy (a), the corresponding frequency spectra (b), and the trajectory constructed from y-velocity located in the shear layer at y = 0 and at streamwise positions x = 3 5 , x = 4 5 (c) are reported.
Figure 10: Reynolds stresses, ⟨u′u′⟩ component. Uncontrolled flow (a) and controlled flow (b).
Figure 11: Reynolds stresses, ⟨v′v′⟩ component. Uncontrolled flow (a) and controlled flow (b).

Figure 12: Evolution of the cost function for the EnVar-based optimisation algorithm for different numbers of ensemble members. Four ensemble sizes are considered (5, 10, 20, and 40 members), represented by − · −, −−, —, and · · · , respectively. Results are reported for both the EnVar Gauss–Newton method (black lines) and the EnVar L-BFGS-B method (red lines).
Figure 13: Optimisation of the forcing shape, EnVar using 5 ensemble members (a), EnVar using 10 ensemble members (b), EnVar using 20 ensemble members (c), EnVar using 40 ensemble members (d)
Figure 14: Re = 14000. Streamwise velocity component of mean flow; vorticity field.
Figure 15: Evolution of the cost function. − EnVar Gauss–Newton; − · − EnVar L-BFGS-B.
Figure 16: Optimisation of the forcing shape, obtained with EnVar Gauss–Newton (a) and the EnVar L-BFGS-B (b).

Figure 17: Comparative characterization of quasi-periodic flow dynamics under controlled and uncontrolled conditions. The uncontrolled flow is shown in blue, whereas the controlled flow obtained is shown in red. The time evolution of the global kinetic energy (a), the corresponding frequency spectra (b), and the trajectory constructed from y-velocity located in the shear layer at y = 0 and at streamwise positions x = 3 5 , x = 4 5 (c) are reported.
Figure 18: Reynolds stresses, ⟨u′u′⟩ component. Uncontrolled flow (a) and controlled flow (b).
Figure 19: Reynolds stresses, ⟨v′v′⟩ component. Uncontrolled flow (a) and controlled flow (b).
Figure A.20: Normalized error as a function of ensemble size N for different spatial correlation coefficients.
Figure B.22: Estimated uncertainties normalized, Re = 6250, EnVar using 20 ensemble members (a), EnVar using 40 ensemble members (b)
Figure B.21: Estimated uncertainties, Re = 6250, EnVar using 5 ensemble members (a), EnVar using 10 ensemble members (b)
